title: Demand and supply curves
paperName: Cambridge International AS Level Economics (P1 & P2)
summary: 9708 AS microeconomics — demand, supply, market equilibrium, and curve shifts with GeoGebra supply and demand simulation.
learningObjectives: Explain the laws of demand and supply.
Draw and interpret demand and supply curves.
Analyse effects of curve shifts on equilibrium.

simpleExplanation.title: Buyers meet sellers
simpleExplanation.summary: Demand shows how much consumers will buy at each price; supply shows how much firms will sell. Where they cross, the market clears.
steps: Demand curve slopes down — higher price, lower quantity demanded (ceteris paribus).
Supply curve slopes up — higher price, higher quantity supplied.
Equilibrium: no tendency for price to change — Qd = Qs.
Shift demand or supply — find new equilibrium P and Q.

sections.content: **Demand and supply** are the core 9708 micro model. Every market analysis question starts here — master **shifts vs movements along** before elasticity (2.2).

items: Movement along = price change; shift = non-price factor changed
Demand shifters: income, tastes, substitutes, complements, population
Supply shifters: costs, technology, taxes/subsidies, number of firms
Always label axes P and Q; mark equilibrium clearly

sections.question: Income rises for a normal good. Show the effect on equilibrium P and Q.
sections.solution:
Normal good → **demand shifts right** (at each price, Qd higher).

New equilibrium: **higher P** and **higher Q**.

Supply unchanged in short run.

sections.content: Draw **two equilibria** (E₁ and E₂) with arrows showing curve shifts. Cambridge awards diagram marks separately from written explanation.
